tools_used: web fetch of a supremecourt.gov slip opinion
helpful: statpack CVSG and relist signal cuts — the CVSG bucket was the anchor for this cell
provisioned petition/BIO/QP text — the BIO's vehicle arguments drove the downward adjustment
CourtListener MCP for establishing the holding of the related decided case the petition was held for
gaps: fedcourts query returned zero rows for every topic tried (transgender, prison, equal protection, strip search, inmate) — either topic matching is too strict or the corpus lacks topical priors for modern cert petitions; a query-by-signal ability (e.g. 'resolved CVSG petitions with their dispositions') would have been more useful than topic search
statpack CVSG bucket doesn't say whether GVRs are counted in 'granted', which matters for the binary axis on CVSG-heavy cells
the prompt references a 'Segment base rate by salience band' statpack section that is not present in the committed statpack.md
notes: Slip-opinion syllabus extraction needed an ad-hoc pypdf install; a small provisioned PDF-text helper would make opinion retrieval cheaper.
